skill.name: technical-blog-writing
skill.description: Technical blog post writing with structure, code examples, and developer audience conventions. Covers post types, code formatting, explanation depth, and developer-specific engagement patterns. Use for: engineering blogs, dev tutorials, technical writing, developer content, documentation posts. Triggers: technical blog, dev blog, engineering blog, technical writing, developer tutorial, tech post, code tutorial, programming blog, developer content, technical article, engineering post, coding tutorial, technical content
skill.summary: Technical blog post writing with structure, code examples, and developer audience conventions. Cover...
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All 52 static findings are false positives. The skill is a writing guide containing markdown documentation with example CLI commands. External commands are documented usage examples, not executed code. Network URLs point to the tool's own website (inference.sh). Weak crypto and system reconnaissance findings triggered on benign keywords like 'MD5' (checksum verification), 'fix' (fixing problems), and 'How We Did It' (blog structure template). This is a safe, documentation-only skill with no executable code.

content.user_title: Write Technical Blog Posts for Developers
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to write engaging technical content that resonates with their audience. This skill provides structured templates, writing rules, and post type guides for creating developer-focused blog posts that get read and shared.
actual_capabilities: Structure technical blog posts using proven templates (tutorial, deep dive, postmortem, benchmark, architecture)
Apply developer-specific writing rules for voice, tone, and code examples
Format code blocks with proper language identifiers, error handling, and realistic examples
Create diagrams and visuals using CLI tools for architecture and benchmark visualizations
Distribute content to developer platforms (Dev.to, Hashnode, Hacker News, Reddit)
limitations: Does not generate full blog posts - provides guidance and templates only
No direct publishing to external platforms - only shows CLI commands for distribution
Assumes basic knowledge of the technical topic being written about

use_cases.title: Write a technical tutorial
use_cases.description: Create a step-by-step tutorial with runnable code examples, prerequisites, and next steps

use_cases.title: Document a system architecture
use_cases.description: Explain how a system was built with diagrams, trade-offs, and decision rationale

use_cases.title: Publish a postmortem report
use_cases.description: Write an incident report with timeline, root cause analysis, and prevention measures

prompt_templates.title: Basic blog structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a technical blog post about [TOPIC] using the tutorial structure. Include: what we built, prerequisites, step-by-step implementation, complete code, and next steps. Target audience: [LEVEL] developers.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture deep dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an architecture blog post about [SYSTEM]. Explain the problem, constraints, options considered, final architecture with diagram description, trade-offs accepted, and lessons learned.

prompt_templates.title: Benchmark comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a benchmark comparison post for [TOOL_A] vs [TOOL_B]. Include methodology, test results with numbers, analysis, and recommendation with caveats.

prompt_templates.title: Postmortem report
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a postmortem for [INCIDENT]. Include: summary with impact and duration, timeline of events, root cause analysis, fix implemented, prevention measures, and lessons learned.

output_examples.input: Write a tutorial about building a REST API with Node.js
output_examples.output:
# Building a REST API with Node.js

**TL;DR:** Learn to build a production-ready REST API with Express, including validation, error handling, and testing.

## What We Are Building
A REST API with CRUD operations for a task management app...

## Prerequisites
- Node.js 20+
- Basic JavaScript knowledge
- MongoDB (local or Atlas)

## Step 1: Project Setup

```bash
npm init -y
npm install express mongoose joi
```

...

output_examples.input: Write an architecture post about our caching layer
output_examples.output:
# How We Built Our Multi-Layer Caching System

**TL;DR:** We implemented a 3-tier caching layer reducing API latency from 800ms to 90ms.

## The Problem
Our API was experiencing high latency during peak traffic...

## Constraints
- Budget: $X/month
- Latency target: <100ms p99
- Consistency: Eventual for non-critical data

## Architecture Chosen
[Diagram: Client → CDN → Redis → Database]

...

best_practices: Start with the end result - show what reader will build or learn first
Include runnable code examples with error handling and pinned dependencies
Be specific with numbers - cite benchmarks, percentages, and concrete outcomes
anti_patterns: Using filler phrases like 'In today's fast-paced world' or 'Simply do X'
Burying the main point under long introductions before reaching the content
Omitting trade-offs and limitations - developers trust honest, balanced content

faq.question: What is the ideal word count for a technical blog post?
faq.answer: Word count varies by type: quick tips (500-800 words), tutorials (1,500-3,000), deep dives (2,000-4,000), and architecture posts (2,000-3,500).

faq.question: How do I make code examples more developer-friendly?
faq.answer: Make every code block runnable, include error handling, use realistic variable names, pin dependency versions, and show the output after the code.

faq.question: What platforms should I cross-post technical content to?
faq.answer: Primary: your own blog (own your content). Cross-post: Dev.to and Hashnode with canonical URLs. Share: Hacker News and Reddit (follow community rules).

faq.question: How do I determine the right explanation depth?
faq.answer: Match depth to audience signals: 'Getting started' needs basics explained, 'Advanced patterns' skips fundamentals, 'How we built X' assumes technical expertise.

faq.question: What should I include in a TL;DR section?
faq.answer: A 2-3 sentence summary with the key takeaway, specific outcome numbers, and a link to the detailed content.

faq.question: How do I create diagrams for technical posts?
faq.answer: Use sequence diagrams for flows, box-and-arrow for architecture, flowcharts for decisions, and bar charts for performance comparisons.
